data.title: Man Yue Technology sets June 2026 AGM to approve accounts, director re-elections and share issue mandate
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Man Yue Technology Holdings Ltd ( (HK:0894) ) has issued an update.

Man Yue Technology Holdings Ltd has called its annual general meeting for 26 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will receive and adopt the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the related directors’ and auditor’s reports. The agenda includes re-election of executive director Chan Yu Ching, Eugene and executive director Chan Lok Yan, Lorraine, as well as long-serving independent non-executive director Lo Kwok Kwei, David, together with authorisation for the board to determine directors’ remuneration and the re-appointment of KPMG as auditor.

Shareholders will also vote on a general mandate allowing the board to issue additional shares or dispose of treasury shares, subject to a cap of 15 per cent of the existing share capital and in line with Hong Kong’s listing requirements. This mandate, if approved, would enhance the company’s financial flexibility for potential capital raising or corporate actions over the coming year, while also reaffirming corporate governance arrangements through director re-elections and auditor continuity.

More about Man Yue Technology Holdings Ltd

Man Yue Technology Holdings Ltd is a Hong Kong–listed company incorporated in Bermuda and traded on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The group operates in the technology and electronics sector, with its shares subject to the Listing Rules of Hong Kong, and is governed by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors.

Average Trading Volume: 382,360

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$369.1M

data.title: Hainan Meilan Airport Sets 2026 AGM, Proposes No 2025 Final Dividend and New Share Mandate
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Hainan Meilan International Airport Company ( (HK:0357) ) is now available.

Hainan Meilan International Airport Company Limited has convened its annual general meeting for 21 May 2026 in Haikou to review the board and supervisory committee work reports, approve the audited financial statements for 2025 and set the 2026 remuneration for directors and supervisors. The board is also proposing not to distribute a final dividend for 2025, a move that preserves cash within the business and may signal a focus on funding operations or investment needs over near-term shareholder payouts.

Shareholders will vote on reappointing Pan-China Certified Public Accountants LLP as auditors for another term, maintaining continuity in the company’s audit oversight and financial reporting. In addition, the board is seeking a renewed general mandate to issue up to 20% of the existing domestic shares and 20% of the H shares, subject to PRC laws and regulatory approval, which would give the company flexibility to raise equity capital and adjust its capital structure if required.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0357) stock is a Sell with a HK$7.30 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Hainan Meilan International Airport Company stock, see the HK:0357 Stock Forecast page.

More about Hainan Meilan International Airport Company

Hainan Meilan International Airport Company Limited is a PRC-based joint stock company that operates Meilan International Airport in Haikou, Hainan Province. Listed in Hong Kong, it provides airport-related services and infrastructure, serving as a key regional aviation hub supporting passenger and cargo traffic for Hainan’s tourism- and trade-focused economy.

The company’s operations encompass airport management, ground handling and associated commercial services to airlines and travelers.

YTD Price Performance: -43.36%

Average Trading Volume: 860,809

Logory Logistics Technology Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2482) ) has issued an announcement.

Logory Logistics Technology Co., Ltd. has called its annual general meeting for May 28, 2026, in Hefei, where shareholders will review and approve the 2025 annual report, board and supervisory board work reports, the 2025 profit distribution plan, and the re-appointment of auditors. The meeting will also consider extensive board and supervisory board reshuffles, a remuneration plan for the new term, approvals for 2026 bank credit, loans and guarantees to wholly owned subsidiaries, and a mandate authorizing the board to issue up to 20% additional H shares, moves that collectively aim to strengthen governance, funding flexibility and operational capacity for future growth.

Logory Logistics Technology Co., Ltd. is a logistics technology company based in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong. Water Intelligence ( (GB:WATR) ) has issued an announcement.

Water Intelligence has repurchased 4,000 ordinary shares at 258 pence each under its shareholder-approved buyback authority, executing the transaction on 24 April 2026 via the London Stock Exchange. The move forms part of the company’s ongoing capital management strategy, with all shares bought back to be held in treasury.

Following the latest buyback, Water Intelligence now holds 656,650 ordinary shares in treasury, and the total number of voting rights stands at 18,911,038 shares across its admitted Ordinary Shares and non-admitted B Ordinary Shares. The updated voting rights figure provides investors with a new reference point for calculating disclosure thresholds under UK transparency rules.

Geely Automobile Holdings has called its annual general meeting for 1 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the directors’ report, audited financial statements and auditor’s report for the financial year ended 31 December 2025. Jolimark Holdings ( (HK:2028) ) has issued an update.

Jolimark Holdings has convened its annual general meeting for 28 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will be asked to adopt the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025, re-elect two executive directors and one independent non-executive director, and approve the remuneration of both the board and the external auditors. The meeting will also seek shareholder authorisation for the board to issue new shares and resell treasury shares up to 20% of the company’s issued share capital, a standard mandate that preserves financial flexibility for future fundraising, capital management and potential strategic initiatives without requiring separate approvals for each transaction.

If approved, the re-election of directors will maintain continuity in Jolimark’s leadership and governance structure, while the re-appointment of Gary Cheng CPA Limited as auditors reinforces consistency in the company’s audit oversight. The broad share issuance mandate, bounded by Hong Kong listing rules and the company’s articles, could affect future capital structure and shareholder dilution, underscoring the importance for investors to monitor how and when the board may exercise these powers over the coming year.

More about Jolimark Holdings

Jolimark Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and traded under stock code 2028. The group operates through a corporate structure that includes subsidiaries, with its governance and reporting overseen by a board of directors and external auditors based in Hong Kong.

data.title: Maven Renovar VCT offers 12% tender ahead of shift to AIM Plus strategy
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Amati AIM ( (GB:MRV) ).

Maven Renovar VCT PLC has launched a tender offer to return up to 12 per cent of its issued share capital to investors, giving shareholders a cash exit ahead of a proposed shift to an “AIM Plus” strategy. The offer, to be voted on at a May general meeting, allows eligible investors to tender all their shares, though purchases will be capped at 12 per cent of the register and may be scaled back if over-subscribed.

Shares accepted under the tender will be bought at a 3.5 per cent discount to net asset value, a level the board expects to be at a premium to the prevailing market price and to avoid material NAV dilution for those remaining invested. The buyback, executed via Shore Capital and funded from distributable reserves, underscores the company’s effort to balance strategic repositioning with liquidity for dissenting holders while excluding investors in certain restricted overseas jurisdictions for regulatory reasons.

More about Amati AIM

Maven Renovar VCT PLC is a UK-listed venture capital trust investing in smaller, growth-oriented companies, with a focus on quoted and AIM-related strategies. The board is considering shifting to an “AIM Plus” investment approach, targeting an expanded universe of AIM and complementary assets to enhance returns for shareholders.

Average Trading Volume: 20,630

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.title: icetana AI lifts recurring revenue and expands market reach with on-premises security AI launch
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

iCetana Ltd. ( (AU:ICE) ) has issued an announcement.

icetana AI reported strong growth for the March 2026 quarter, with annual recurring revenue rising to $2.6 million, up 12% quarter-on-quarter and 51% year-on-year, and total quarterly revenue increasing 40% year-on-year to $607,000. The company’s net operating cash outflow widened to $1.2 million and its quarter-end cash balance declined to $1.0 million, though a further $705,000 was received in April from a customer, easing near-term liquidity pressure.

Management attributed the ARR momentum to new customer wins and expansion sales, supported by heightened safety concerns driving demand for on-site protection. The launch of Antara Core, an on-premises AI security workflow suite, opens a significant market segment that cloud-based AI cannot serve, potentially strengthening icetana AI’s competitive position as it seeks to scale revenue, deepen customer relationships and broaden its global partner network.

More about iCetana Ltd.

icetana Ltd., trading as icetana AI, develops self-learning security artificial intelligence software aimed at large-scale surveillance networks. Its products deliver AI-powered security workflows, including the newly launched Antara Core on-premises solution, targeting organisations with strict data sovereignty requirements and growing global demand for private, scalable AI in security operations.

Current Market Cap: A$18.61M

IG Group Holdings ( (GB:IGG) ) has shared an announcement.

IG Group Holdings has continued its share buyback activity, purchasing 162,551 ordinary shares between 20 and 24 April 2026 through Morgan Stanley & Co. International at volume-weighted average prices ranging from 1,509.04p to 1,536.53p. The company intends to hold these shares in treasury, supporting capital management flexibility and potentially enhancing earnings per share over time.

Following the latest transactions, IG Group now holds 29,859,755 ordinary shares in treasury and has 331,698,113 ordinary shares in issue, excluding treasury stock. Since 1 April 2026, the group has repurchased a total of 532,376 shares at an aggregate cost of about £7.95m, underscoring an ongoing commitment to returning capital to shareholders and managing its equity base.
